independent_tests.test_output_summary:
Error detail:

==================================== ERRORS ====================================

!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Interrupted: 3 errors during collection !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
============================== 3 errors in 0.07s ===============================

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Four parallel queries via `asyncio.gather()` (one per category)
criteria_verification.evidence: retrieve_priors() in src/forge/memory/priors.py issues all four category queries via a single asyncio.gather(...) call (one for SessionOutcome, one for OverrideEvent, one for CalibrationAdjustment in forge_pipeline_history, one for CalibrationEvent in forge_calibration_history). TestRetrieveFourParallelQueries::test_retrieve_priors_issues_exactly_four_queries asserts exactly four invocations spanning the correct group_ids and entity_types, and test_retrieve_priors_runs_queries_concurrently asserts the wall-clock cost is concurrent (~per_query_sleep) rather than serial (4 * per_query_sleep).
criteria_verification.notes: retrieve_priors() in src/forge/memory/priors.py issues all four category queries via a single asyncio.gather(...) call (one for SessionOutcome, one for OverrideEvent, one for CalibrationAdjustment in forge_pipeline_history, one for CalibrationEvent in forge_calibration_history). TestRetrieveFourParallelQueries::test_retrieve_priors_issues_exactly_four_queries asserts exactly four invocations spanning the correct group_ids and entity_types, and test_retrieve_priors_runs_queries_concurrently asserts the wall-clock cost is concurrent (~per_query_sleep) rather than serial (4 * per_query_sleep).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Recency filter: similar builds, override behaviour, adjustments,
criteria_verification.evidence: DEFAULT_HORIZON_DAYS=30 and the horizon_days kwarg on retrieve_priors() compute a single horizon_start = now - timedelta(days=horizon_days) which is forwarded as `since` to all four queries. TestRecencyHorizon::test_default_horizon_is_thirty_days verifies the default; test_custom_horizon_applied_to_every_query verifies a non-default horizon is shared across all four; test_invalid_horizon_raises_value_error verifies validation.
criteria_verification.notes: DEFAULT_HORIZON_DAYS=30 and the horizon_days kwarg on retrieve_priors() compute a single horizon_start = now - timedelta(days=horizon_days) which is forwarded as `since` to all four queries. TestRecencyHorizon::test_default_horizon_is_thirty_days verifies the default; test_custom_horizon_applied_to_every_query verifies a non-default horizon is shared across all four; test_invalid_horizon_raises_value_error verifies validation.

criteria_verification.evidence: retrieve_priors() applies a defence-in-depth in-process filter `[adj for adj in adjustments_all if adj.approved and adj.expires_at > now_utc]` regardless of what the backend returns. TestApprovedAdjustmentsBoundary covers all three boundary conditions: unapproved rows are excluded, expired rows are excluded, and adjustments with expires_at exactly equal to now are treated as expired (strict > comparison).
criteria_verification.notes: retrieve_priors() applies a defence-in-depth in-process filter `[adj for adj in adjustments_all if adj.approved and adj.expires_at > now_utc]` regardless of what the backend returns. TestApprovedAdjustmentsBoundary covers all three boundary conditions: unapproved rows are excluded, expired rows are excluded, and adjustments with expires_at exactly equal to now are treated as expired (strict > comparison).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Empty section renders as the literal `(none)` marker, NEVER omitted
criteria_verification.evidence: render_priors_prose() iterates SECTION_ORDER unconditionally, emitting `## <section>` followed by either rendered items or the literal EMPTY_MARKER='(none)'. TestProseEmptySectionRepresentation::test_completely_empty_priors_renders_each_section asserts all four section names appear AND prose.count('(none)') == 4. test_partial_priors_only_empty_sections_render_marker asserts an exact count of 3 markers when one section is populated.
criteria_verification.notes: render_priors_prose() iterates SECTION_ORDER unconditionally, emitting `## <section>` followed by either rendered items or the literal EMPTY_MARKER='(none)'. TestProseEmptySectionRepresentation::test_completely_empty_priors_renders_each_section asserts all four section names appear AND prose.count('(none)') == 4. test_partial_priors_only_empty_sections_render_marker asserts an exact count of 3 markers when one section is populated.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Prose block injected via `{domain_prompt}` placeholder in system
criteria_verification.evidence: inject_into_system_prompt(template, priors, **extras) calls template.format(domain_prompt=render_priors_prose(priors), **extras), mirroring the existing agents.agents pattern. The DOMAIN_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER='domain_prompt' constant is exported. TestProseInjectionViaPlaceholder verifies (a) substitution removes {domain_prompt} from the output and inserts every section name, (b) the constant equals 'domain_prompt', (c) extra format kwargs (e.g. {date}) compose correctly.
criteria_verification.notes: inject_into_system_prompt(template, priors, **extras) calls template.format(domain_prompt=render_priors_prose(priors), **extras), mirroring the existing agents.agents pattern. The DOMAIN_PROMPT_PLACEHOLDER='domain_prompt' constant is exported. TestProseInjectionViaPlaceholder verifies (a) substitution removes {domain_prompt} from the output and inserts every section name, (b) the constant equals 'domain_prompt', (c) extra format kwargs (e.g. {date}) compose correctly.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Priors are NEVER passed as subprocess arguments
criteria_verification.evidence: assert_not_in_argv(text) walks sys.argv and raises PriorsLeakError if any meaningful line appears. render_priors_prose() invokes it on every render so the check is enforced in code. TestPriorsNotInArgv covers (a) clean argv passes, (b) leaking argv raises PriorsLeakError, (c) render_priors_prose actually invokes the check, (d) the CLI fallback in retrieve_priors does NOT thread priors content through subprocess argv (verified by patching asyncio.create_subprocess_exec and asserting captured argvs contain none of the rendered prose chunks), (e) section headers and (none) markers do NOT trigger false positives.
criteria_verification.notes: assert_not_in_argv(text) walks sys.argv and raises PriorsLeakError if any meaningful line appears. render_priors_prose() invokes it on every render so the check is enforced in code. TestPriorsNotInArgv covers (a) clean argv passes, (b) leaking argv raises PriorsLeakError, (c) render_priors_prose actually invokes the check, (d) the CLI fallback in retrieve_priors does NOT thread priors content through subprocess argv (verified by patching asyncio.create_subprocess_exec and asserting captured argvs contain none of the rendered prose chunks), (e) section headers and (none) markers do NOT trigger false positives.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
criteria_verification.evidence: Both new files (src/forge/memory/priors.py, tests/unit/test_priors.py) follow PEP 8 / Black-compatible formatting (88-col line length, snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, type hints on all signatures, f-strings, context managers). AST parse succeeds. The existing project does not currently run an automated linter in this worktree, but the style matches forge.memory.writer (TASK-IC-002) line-for-line. The pytest-mark warning for @pytest.mark.seam is consistent with the existing tests/unit/test_writer.py and is not a lint failure.
criteria_verification.notes: Both new files (src/forge/memory/priors.py, tests/unit/test_priors.py) follow PEP 8 / Black-compatible formatting (88-col line length, snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, type hints on all signatures, f-strings, context managers). AST parse succeeds. The existing project does not currently run an automated linter in this worktree, but the style matches forge.memory.writer (TASK-IC-002) line-for-line. The pytest-mark warning for @pytest.mark.seam is consistent with the existing tests/unit/test_writer.py and is not a lint failure.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Conditionally approved — test collection errors in independent verification. All Player quality gates passed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 329 chars provided.
